summary: Recent updates in the 2D graphics area of Firefox have focused on improving font handling and performance. These changes include better management of font entries through enhanced reference counting, which helps prevent errors related to memory allocation. A significant adjustment involves applying **letter-spacing for uppercase transformations of the es-zet character**, ensuring proper typographic representation. Additionally, optimizations around text processing have been made, including the ability to skip unnecessary checks for simple Latin text, leading to faster rendering and an overall smoother browsing experience. These enhancements collectively contribute to a more efficient and visually appealing presentation of text on websites.
